content.full_text:
87

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 6, 1989

Kevin Sloat
Administrative Assistant to
Senator Marian Bergeson
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-164

Dear Mr. Sloat:

You have requested advice concerning the responsibilities of
Senator Marian Bergeson under the limitation on gifts and
honoraria provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ This letter
confirms that your letter dated March 13, 1989, accurately
summarizes the advice I provided to you regarding the
Senator's acceptance of an honorarium, and payment for actual
travel expenses and necessary accommodations directly in
connection with a speech she will be giving in Washington.

Please contact me at 322-5901 if you have any further
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SELECT COMMITTEES:
CHAIRMAN,
PLANNING FOR
CALIFORNIA'S GROWTH
INFANT & CHILD CARE
JOINT CHAIRS,
BORDER ISSUES: DRUG
TRAFFICKING & CONTRABAND
STATE TASK FORCE ON
CALIFORNIA-MEXICO RELATIONS

STANDING COMMITTEES:
CHAIRMAN,
LOCAL GOVERNMENT
VICE CHAIRMAN,
BUDGET & FISCAL REVIEW
MEMBER, NATURAL
RESOURCES
TRANSPORTATION

SUBCOMMITTEES:
CHAIRMAN,
REDEVELOPMENT OF
UNINCORPORATED SUBDIVISIONS
CHAIRMAN,
WATER MARKETING
AGRICULTURAL DRAINAGE
PRACTICES

California State Senate
MARIAN BERGESON
SENATOR
THIRTY-SEVENTH DISTRICT

CHAIRMAN
LOCAL GOVERNMENT COMMITTEE

March 13, 1989

Ms. Lilly Spitz
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Lilly:

Thank you for your prompt response to our request for
clarification on P.F.P.C. guidelines regarding the acceptance of
travel expenses exclusive of an honorarium for an out-of-state
speech.

Incidentally, the Senator's topic is "Growth in the 90's", and
the Senator will be speaking on related legislation in the area.
This subject matter should fit within the category of the
governmental process.  My understanding based on our telephone
conversation today is that it is legal for Senator Bergeson to
accept travel expenses to Washington, in addition, to a $1,000
honorarium.

We would appreciate receiving a written response confirming these
facts.  Again, thank you for your assistance.

Cordially,

Kevin Sloat
Administrative Assistant to
Senator Marian Bergeson

☐ CAPITOL OFFICE                          STATE CAPITOL, SACRAMENTO CA 95814                    TELEPHONE: (916) 445-4961
☐ ORANGE COUNTY                    140 NEWPORT CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 150, NEWPORT BEACH CA 92660             TELEPHONE: (714) 644-8140
☐ IMPERIAL VALLEY                        101 AIRPORT ROAD, SUITE C, IMPERIAL CA 92251               TELEPHONE: (619) 353-8244
☐ WESTERN RIVERSIDE                                                                                                                              TELEPHONE: (714) 676-6908
☐ NORTHEASTERN SAN DIEGO COUNTIES

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 15, 1989


Kevin Sloat
Administrative Assistant to
Senator Marian Bergeson
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-164

Dear Mr. Sloat:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on March 14, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to Lilly Spitz for response.  If you have any
questions, you may contact Ms. Spitz directly at (916) 322-5901.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5[illegible]
